name: Chinese Pancakes B1
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the flour in a mixing bowl and make a well in the center.
2. Add the boiling water, stirring quickly with a fork until a rough dough forms. It shouldn't be too sticky; you may not need all the water.
3. Turn the dough onto lightly floured board and knead until smooth (10 minutes), adding more flour if necessary.
4. Place the dough in the mixing bowl and cover. Allow to rest for 30 minutes.
5. Moisten hands with sesame oil.
6. Pull off golf ball-sized pieces of dough and flatten them.
7. Brush the top of each flattened piece with sesame oil, place another flattened piece on top.
8. With a rolling pin, roll the pair together into a 6-8 inch circle.
9. Repeat with all the dough.
10. Place each set of pancakes on a baking pan. Bake in a moderate oven (325-350°F) for 1-2 minutes until one side puffs up.
11. Flip and bake on the other side for another minute or two.
12. Pull the pancakes apart while still warm.
13. Let them cool.
14. Fold each pancake into quarters, place on aluminum foil.
15. Wrap each package tightly and steam for 5-10 minutes at a hot temperature.
